title: Accelerated Evolving Set Processes for Local PageRank Computation

repos.name: Rick7117__aesp-local-pagerank
repos.evidence: README states 'implementation and experimental code for our paper "Accelerated Evolving Set Processes for Local PageRank Computation" submitted to NIPS 2025' and cites arXiv:2510.08010; repo owner 'Rick7117' matches first author Binbin Huang (bbhuang24@m.fudan.edu.cn, 'Rick' is a plausible handle); ppr_solver.py implements the paper's named algorithms aespAPPR and aespLocGD alongside baselines appr/locGD; plot scripts match paper figures (convergence, vol/gamma, R-vs-alpha); the audit smoke-tested aespAPPR/aespLocGD on as-skitter and confirmed operation counts match paper Table 3 order-of-magnitude.

rationale: The single cloned repo (Rick7117/aesp-local-pagerank) is unambiguously this paper's own implementation: the README explicitly names the paper title and NeurIPS 2025 venue, links to arXiv:2510.08010, and the codebase implements the paper's headline AESP methods (aespAPPR, aespLocGD) in ppr_solver.py. The audit's findings (missing datasets, no results/*.npz, dead /mnt paths, generic README template, no dependency spec) are all legitimate reproducibility defects written against the correct repo. No non-core repo was cloned. The audit correctly classified this paper as source-present.
audit_impact: None — all findings were written against the correct core repo. The verdict and finding set require no correction.
recommended_action: No action needed on repo provenance. Findings stand as legitimate reproducibility defects.
